title: Request transfer credit verification
subject: Transfer credit verification — [Student ID]
body:
Hello [Advisor name],

I'm writing to confirm the status of my transfer credits from [previous institution]. I expected the following to appear on my Advisement Report:

- [Course code 1] → Concordia [Course code 1 equivalent]
- [Course code 2] → Concordia [Course code 2 equivalent]

Could you please confirm when these will be processed, or let me know if anything additional is needed from my side?

Thank you,
[Your name]
[Student ID] · [Program]

title: Request prerequisite waiver
subject: Prerequisite waiver request — [Course code]
body:
Dear Professor [Name],

I'd like to request a prerequisite waiver to register in [Course code] for [Term].

The official prerequisite is [Prereq code], which I have not yet completed. However, I have completed [equivalent/related course] which covers similar material. I am also currently in [related course] and would be taking [Course code] concurrently.

Would you be willing to sign a prerequisite override? I can provide my transcript or course outlines on request.

Thank you,
[Your name]
[Student ID] · [Program]

title: Request permission to register (course full)
subject: Registration permission — [Course code] [Section]
body:
Hello,

I'm trying to register for [Course code] [Section] in [Term], but the section is full. This course is on the critical path for my graduation in [Expected grad term] and I would like to be added.

I have completed all listed prerequisites:
- [Prereq 1] ✓
- [Prereq 2] ✓

Is there a waitlist I can join, or could I be added to the section? I'm available to come to the department office in person if needed.

Thank you,
[Your name]
[Student ID]

title: EWT scheduling / status clarification
subject: EWT scheduling for [Term] — [Student ID]
body:
Hello,

Could you confirm the next available sitting of the Engineering Writing Test (EWT)?

My plan is to take ENCS 282 in [Target term] if I pass the EWT, or otherwise register for ENCS 272 first. I'd like to align my registration before the add/drop deadline of [Date].

Thank you,
[Your name]
[Student ID]

title: Request late DISC (withdrawal without academic penalty)
subject: Late DISC request — [Course code] [Term]
body:
Hello,

I'd like to request a late DISC for [Course code] this term. The standard drop deadline has passed, but my current circumstances ([brief reason — health, family, etc.]) have made continuing unmanageable.

I understand DISC appears on the transcript but does not affect GPA. I've reviewed the implications with [Counsellor / professor] and would like to proceed.

What documentation do you need from me?

Thank you,
[Your name]
[Student ID]

title: Co-op application status
subject: Co-op application status — [Student ID]
body:
Hello,

I applied to the SOEN Co-op program on [Date]. Could you confirm:

1. Whether my application has been reviewed
2. The expected timeline for the decision
3. Any additional documents you need from me

Thank you,
[Your name]
[Student ID] · [Program]

title: Graduation application + audit
subject: Graduation application audit — Spring [Year]
body:
Hello,

I'm applying for [Spring/Fall] [Year] convocation and would like to confirm I'm on track.

My Advisement Report shows:
- Total credits: [X] / 120
- Outstanding: [list any remaining courses]
- Deficiencies cleared: [Yes/No]

Could you confirm that everything is in order, or flag anything missing?

Thank you,
[Your name]
[Student ID] · [Program]

title: Two-course schedule conflict resolution
subject: Schedule conflict — [Course A] and [Course B]
body:
Hello,

I have a schedule conflict between [Course A] [Section] and [Course B] [Section] in [Term]. Both are required for my plan.

Options I've considered:
1. Different section of [Course A] (if available)
2. Different section of [Course B] (if available)
3. Switching one of them to [alternative term]

Do you know which option is most realistic, or is there a section override path?

Thank you,
[Your name]
[Student ID]

title: Reference letter request
subject: Reference letter request — [Application / Job]
body:
Dear Professor [Name],

I'm applying for [position / program] with a deadline of [Date]. I took your [Course code] in [Term] and earned [Grade] — I learned a lot from [specific topic / project].

Would you be willing to write a reference letter on my behalf? I can send you my CV, the application details, and a short summary of what I'm hoping to highlight. I'd need the letter submitted to [Submission portal / email] by [Date].

Thank you for considering,
[Your name]
[Student ID]
